message:
fix(llm,doctor): align error messages with test contracts

Three related error-message fixes — the messages had drifted out of
sync with the assertions after Bedrock and CLI providers were added.

1. doctor.runSelfTest LLM System check
   Test: "reports fail when no API keys are set"
     expects: status "fail" AND message contains "No API keys configured"
   Previous message: "No LLM provider configured (set an API key or install ...)"
   Now: "No API keys configured (set an API key env var or install ...)"

2. getApiKey() — unknown-provider error
   Test: "includes supported providers in unknown provider error"
     calls getApiKey("bedrock") and expects message contains
     "Supported providers: openai, anthropic, google, ollama"
   Previous: Bedrock and CLI silently returned "" from getApiKey(),
     so the throw never happened, and when it did fire, the supported
     list included bedrock/cli themselves — wrong for callers that
     want to know which providers can actually resolve an API key.
   Now: getApiKey() only short-circuits for ollama (a documented
     no-API-key provider). For bedrock/cli/unknown, it throws with
     the canonical supported-providers list. Bedrock and CLI auth
     is still handled in getModel()/llmWithFallback(), which never
     call getApiKey() with those providers (verified in the routing
     logic above getModel's delegation to getApiKey at line 176).

3. llmWithFallback() — no-providers-available error
   Test: "includes setup instructions in no-provider error"
     expects message contains "OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY,
     GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY, or OLLAMA_BASE_URL"
   Previous: comma-separated list had no "or" and was followed by
     ", AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID..." so the substring didn't match.
   Now: reworded to put the four primary env-var-based providers
     first with an Oxford-style "or", then mentions Bedrock and
     CLI as additional options in a second sentence. Preserves all
     original setup information — nothing is dropped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
